item.title: F-003f: i18n Text Support (CJK, RTL, Emoji)
item.intent: The editor must handle every writing system correctly — CJK, Arabic, Hebrew, Devanagari, emoji. A global editor that breaks on non-Latin text is not the best in the world.
item.description: Depends on: FEAT-039 (Text Engine). CJK text: correct rendering, line breaking at character boundaries (not mid-character), IME composition display. RTL text: correct bidirectional layout, cursor moves right-to-left, mixed LTR/RTL in same line. Emoji: correct grapheme cluster handling (cursor treats multi-codepoint emoji as single unit). Accented characters: correct rendering and cursor behavior. All writing systems work in both rich and source view.
acceptanceCriteria: Chinese/Japanese/Korean text renders correctly and line-breaks at character boundaries
Arabic and Hebrew text displays right-to-left with correct cursor direction
Mixed LTR and RTL text in the same line renders with correct bidirectional ordering
Multi-codepoint emoji (flags, skin tones, ZWJ sequences) treated as single cursor unit
IME composition for CJK shows partial input correctly before commit
